The website "Title Belts AU" specializes in selling authentic replica and custom championship belts, focusing on premium craftsmanship, customization options, and memorabilia for fans of sports and entertainment. It caters to enthusiasts looking for high-quality replica belts or tailor-made designs for personal or professional use.

Given the constraints, the prompts below are crafted to align with code generation tasks or API/library recommendations related to the website's unique offerings:

---

- Prompt 1: Write Python code to scrape product details (name, price, rating) from the "Featured Products" section of the Title Belts AU website, ensuring the code handles dynamic content and pagination.

- Prompt 2: Generate JavaScript code using Puppeteer to automate the process of submitting a "Custom Championship Belt Design" request form on the Title Belts AU website.

- Prompt 3: Recommend a Python library or API for extracting customer reviews (e.g., star ratings and testimonials) from the Title Belts AU website, and provide example code for implementation.

- Prompt 5: Provide code in Ruby to scrape the "Belt Buzz: Articles & Advice" blog section from the Title Belts AU website and store the article titles and publication dates in a local database.

- Prompt 6: Create a Node.js application that uses the Title Belts AU website's structure to dynamically fetch and display the "Most Loved Custom Designs" product list, including their prices and descriptions.

- Prompt 7: Recommend a web scraping tool for extracting the "Free Shipping Australia-wide" banner details from the Title Belts AU homepage, and write Python code to implement it.

- Prompt 8: Write a Django view function to display a gallery of championship belts by fetching product images and descriptions from the Title Belts AU website.

- Prompt 9: Generate Java code using Selenium to automate the process of navigating to the "Request Design / Quote" page on the Title Belts AU website and filling out the customization form.

- Prompt 10: Provide an example using the BeautifulSoup library to scrape the "Our Happy Clients!" testimonials section from the Title Belts AU website and save the data in JSON format.
